paper_title: Laboratory and Field Analysis of Arctic Sedimentary Sequences

methods.description: Determination of soil organic matter content using Loss on Ignition (LOI) at 550°C for 3 hours.

steps.description: Soil organic matter content was determined by Loss on Ignition (LOI) at 550°C for 3 hours.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Loss on Ignition at 550°C for 3 hours to determine soil organic matter content

methods.description: Measurement of grain size after pretreatment with hydrogen peroxide to remove organic fractions.

steps.description: Samples were first pretreated with hydrogen peroxide (H2O2) to eliminate the organic fraction.
steps.searchable_summary: pretreat samples with hydrogen peroxide to remove organic material

steps.description: Subsequently analysed with a Malvern Mastersizer 2000.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze grain size using Malvern Mastersizer 2000

methods.description: Dating of plant fragments using AMS 14C Accelerator mass spectrometry to establish a geochronological time frame.

steps.description: Ten samples (plant fragments) from both sedimentary sequences were AMS 14C Accelerator mass spectrometry (AMS) dating.
steps.searchable_summary: perform AMS 14C dating on plant fragments

steps.description: All radiocarbon ages discussed in this research have been converted to calendar ages with the program CALIB 7.0.
steps.searchable_summary: convert radiocarbon ages to calendar ages using CALIB 7.0

methods.description: Acquisition of geochemical composition of fine-grained samples using X-ray fluorescence.

steps.description: The geochemical composition of ten samples (fine-grained fraction (Ø <2 mm)) from different lithostratigraphical units was acquired by X-ray fluorescence (XRF).
steps.searchable_summary: analyze geochemical composition using X-ray fluorescence

methods.description: Calculation of the Chemical Index of Alteration (CIA) using a standard formula.

steps.description: The chemical index of alteration was calculated using the following formula: CIA= (Nesbit and Young, 1982).
steps.searchable_summary: calculate Chemical Index of Alteration using standard formula
